#### Foreign players

Further information: [List of foreign NBA players](/wiki/List_of_foreign_NBA_players "List of foreign NBA players")

Following pioneers like [Vlade Divac](/wiki/Vlade_Divac "Vlade Divac") (Serbia) and [Dražen Petrović](/wiki/Dra%C5%BEen_Petrovi%C4%87 "Dražen Petrović") (Croatia), who joined the NBA in the late 1980s, an increasing number of international players have moved directly from playing elsewhere in the world to starring in the NBA.[[195]](#cite_note-196)[[196]](#cite_note-197) Since 2006, the NBA has faced [EuroLeague](/wiki/EuroLeague "EuroLeague") teams [in exhibition matches](/wiki/NBA_vs._EuroLeague_games "NBA vs. EuroLeague games") in the [NBA Europe Live Tour](/wiki/NBA_Europe_Live_Tour "NBA Europe Live Tour"),[[197]](#cite_note-198) and since 2009, in the [EuroLeague American Tour](/wiki/EuroLeague_American_Tour "EuroLeague American Tour"). On November 9, 2007, when the [Houston Rockets](/wiki/Houston_Rockets "Houston Rockets") with [Yao Ming](/wiki/Yao_Ming "Yao Ming") faced off against the [Milwaukee Bucks](/wiki/Milwaukee_Bucks "Milwaukee Bucks") with [Yi Jianlian](/wiki/Yi_Jianlian "Yi Jianlian"), over 200 million people in China watched on 19 different networks, making it the most-viewed game in NBA history.[[198]](#cite_note-199)

The [2013–14 season](/wiki/2013%E2%80%9314_NBA_season "2013–14 NBA season") opened with a record 92 international players on the opening night rosters, representing 39 countries and comprising over 20 percent of the league.[[199]](#cite_note-Helin2013-200) The NBA defines "international" players as those born outside the 50 United States and Washington, D.C. This means that:

* Players born in U.S. territories such as Puerto Rico and the [U.S. Virgin Islands](/wiki/United_States_Virgin_Islands "United States Virgin Islands"), most notably USVI native [Tim Duncan](/wiki/Tim_Duncan "Tim Duncan"), are counted as "international" even though they are U.S. citizens by birth, and may even have represented the U.S. in international competition (like Duncan).
* U.S.-born players are not counted as "international" even if they were born with citizenship in another country and represent that country internationally, such as [Joakim Noah](/wiki/Joakim_Noah "Joakim Noah"), and [Kosta Koufos](/wiki/Kosta_Koufos "Kosta Koufos").

The beginning of the [2017–18 season](/wiki/2017%E2%80%9318_NBA_season "2017–18 NBA season") saw a record 108 international players representing 42 countries marking 4 consecutive years of at least 100 international players and each team having at least one international player.[[200]](#cite_note-201) In 2018, the [Phoenix Suns](/wiki/Phoenix_Suns "Phoenix Suns") hired Serbian coach [Igor Kokoškov](/wiki/Igor_Koko%C5%A1kov "Igor Kokoškov") as their new head coach, replacing Canadian interim coach [Jay Triano](/wiki/Jay_Triano "Jay Triano"), making Kokoškov the first European coach to become a head coach for a team in the NBA.[[201]](#cite_note-202)

In the [2023–24 season](/wiki/2023%E2%80%9324_NBA_season "2023–24 NBA season"), the Mavericks and the Thunder each had eight international players on their roster.[[202]](#cite_note-international_expansion-203) For seven consecutive seasons from [2018–19](/wiki/2018%E2%80%9319_NBA_season "2018–19 NBA season") to [2024–25](/wiki/2024%E2%80%9325_NBA_season "2024–25 NBA season"), the league's [MVP award](/wiki/NBA_Most_Valuable_Player_Award "NBA Most Valuable Player Award") has been given to an international player.[[203]](#cite_note-204)[[204]](#cite_note-205)

Below is a list of foreign players who have won NBA awards or have otherwise been recognized for their contributions to basketball, either currently or formerly active in the league:

* Dražen Petrović, Croatia – 2002 inductee into the [Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame](/wiki/Naismith_Memorial_Basketball_Hall_of_Fame "Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame"), four-time [Euroscar](/wiki/Euroscar "Euroscar") winner, two-time [Mr. Europa](/wiki/Mr._Europa "Mr. Europa") winner, MVP of the [1986 FIBA World Championship](/wiki/1986_FIBA_World_Championship "1986 FIBA World Championship") and [EuroBasket 1989](/wiki/EuroBasket_1989 "EuroBasket 1989"), two-time Olympic silver medalist, World champion, European champion, [50 Greatest EuroLeague Contributors](/wiki/50_Greatest_EuroLeague_Contributors_(2008) "50 Greatest EuroLeague Contributors (2008)").[[205]](#cite_note-206)
* [Arvydas Sabonis](/wiki/Arvydas_Sabonis "Arvydas Sabonis"), Lithuania – 2011 inductee into the [Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame](/wiki/Naismith_Memorial_Basketball_Hall_of_Fame "Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame"), five-time Euroscar winner, two-time Mr. Europa winner, Olympic gold medalist in [1988](/wiki/Basketball_at_the_1988_Summer_Olympics "Basketball at the 1988 Summer Olympics") with the [Soviet Union](/wiki/Soviet_Union_national_basketball_team "Soviet Union national basketball team") and bronze medalist in [1992](/wiki/Basketball_at_the_1992_Summer_Olympics "Basketball at the 1992 Summer Olympics") and [1996](/wiki/Basketball_at_the_1996_Summer_Olympics "Basketball at the 1996 Summer Olympics") with [Lithuania](/wiki/Lithuania_men%27s_national_basketball_team "Lithuania men's national basketball team"), 1996 NBA All-Rookie First Team, 50 Greatest EuroLeague Contributors.[[206]](#cite_note-207)
* [Šarūnas Marčiulionis](/wiki/%C5%A0ar%C5%ABnas_Mar%C4%8Diulionis "Šarūnas Marčiulionis"), Lithuania – 2014 inductee into the [Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame](/wiki/Naismith_Memorial_Basketball_Hall_of_Fame "Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame"). First player from the Soviet Union and one of the first Europeans to sign a contract with an NBA club and to play solidly in the league, helping to lead the way for the internationalization of the league in the late 1990s.[[207]](#cite_note-208)
* Vlade Divac, Serbia – 2019 inductee into the [Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame](/wiki/Naismith_Memorial_Basketball_Hall_of_Fame "Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame"), two-time Olympic silver medalist, 2001 [NBA All-Star](/wiki/NBA_All-Star "NBA All-Star"), two-time World champion, three-time European champion, 1989 Mr. Europa winner, 50 Greatest EuroLeague Contributors.[[208]](#cite_note-209)
* [Toni Kukoč](/wiki/Toni_Kuko%C4%8D "Toni Kukoč"), Croatia – 2021 inductee into the [Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame](/wiki/Naismith_Memorial_Basketball_Hall_of_Fame "Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame"), three-time NBA champion with the Chicago Bulls (1996, 1997, 1998), 1996 [Sixth Man Award](/wiki/NBA_Sixth_Man_of_the_Year_Award "NBA Sixth Man of the Year Award") winner, named in 2008 as one of the [50 Greatest EuroLeague Contributors](/wiki/50_Greatest_EuroLeague_Contributors "50 Greatest EuroLeague Contributors").[[209]](#cite_note-210)
* [Peja Stojaković](/wiki/Peja_Stojakovi%C4%87 "Peja Stojaković"), Serbia – NBA champion with the Dallas Mavericks (2011), MVP of the [EuroBasket 2001](/wiki/EuroBasket_2001 "EuroBasket 2001"), member of the all-tournament team in the [2002 FIBA World Championship](/wiki/2002_FIBA_World_Championship "2002 FIBA World Championship"), 2001 [Euroscar](/wiki/Euroscar "Euroscar") winner, two-time Mr. Europa winner, two-time [NBA Three-Point Shootout champion](/wiki/NBA_Three-Point_Shootout_champion "NBA Three-Point Shootout champion"), three-time NBA All-Star.
* [Dirk Nowitzki](/wiki/Dirk_Nowitzki "Dirk Nowitzki"), Germany – NBA champion with the Dallas Mavericks (2011), MVP of the [2002 FIBA World Championship](/wiki/2002_FIBA_World_Championship "2002 FIBA World Championship") and [EuroBasket 2005](/wiki/EuroBasket_2005 "EuroBasket 2005"), member of the all-tournament team in the [2002 FIBA World Championship](/wiki/2002_FIBA_World_Championship "2002 FIBA World Championship"), six-time Euroscar winner, 2005 Mr. Europa, two-time [FIBA Europe Player of the Year](/wiki/FIBA_Europe_Player_of_the_Year "FIBA Europe Player of the Year"), 2007 [NBA MVP](/wiki/National_Basketball_Association_Most_Valuable_Player_Award "National Basketball Association Most Valuable Player Award"), 2011 [Bill Russell NBA Finals Most Valuable Player Award](/wiki/Bill_Russell_NBA_Finals_Most_Valuable_Player_Award "Bill Russell NBA Finals Most Valuable Player Award"), 2006 [NBA Three-Point Shootout champion](/wiki/NBA_Three-Point_Shootout_champion "NBA Three-Point Shootout champion") and 14-time NBA All-Star.
* [Hedo Türkoğlu](/wiki/Hedo_T%C3%BCrko%C4%9Flu "Hedo Türkoğlu"), Turkey – 2008 [Most Improved Player Award](/wiki/NBA_Most_Improved_Player_Award "NBA Most Improved Player Award") winner, member of the all-tournament team in the [2010 FIBA World Championship](/wiki/2010_FIBA_World_Championship "2010 FIBA World Championship").
* [Pau Gasol](/wiki/Pau_Gasol "Pau Gasol"), Spain – two-time NBA champion with the Los Angeles Lakers (2009 and 2010), six-time [NBA All-Star](/wiki/NBA_All-Star "NBA All-Star"), 2002 [NBA Rookie of the Year](/wiki/NBA_Rookie_of_the_Year_Award "NBA Rookie of the Year Award"), two-time Mr. Europa, [2006 FIBA World Championship](/wiki/2006_FIBA_World_Championship "2006 FIBA World Championship") MVP, four-time [Euroscar](/wiki/Euroscar_Award "Euroscar Award"), two-time [FIBA Europe Player of the Year](/wiki/FIBA_Europe_Player_of_the_Year_Award "FIBA Europe Player of the Year Award"), MVP of the [EuroBasket 2009](/wiki/EuroBasket_2009 "EuroBasket 2009") and [EuroBasket 2015](/wiki/EuroBasket_2015 "EuroBasket 2015"), winner of the [NBA Citizenship Award](/wiki/J._Walter_Kennedy_Citizenship_Award "J. Walter Kennedy Citizenship Award") in 2012.
* [Andrei Kirilenko](/wiki/Andrei_Kirilenko "Andrei Kirilenko"), Russia – 2004 NBA All-Star, MVP of the [EuroBasket 2007](/wiki/EuroBasket_2007 "EuroBasket 2007"), 2007 FIBA Europe Player of the Year.
* [Tony Parker](/wiki/Tony_Parker "Tony Parker"), France – four-time NBA champion with the San Antonio Spurs, [2007 NBA Finals](/wiki/2007_NBA_Finals "2007 NBA Finals") MVP, six-time NBA All-Star and 2007 Euroscar winner.
* [Manu Ginóbili](/wiki/Manu_Gin%C3%B3bili "Manu Ginóbili"), Argentina – four-time NBA champion with the San Antonio Spurs, 2008 Sixth Man Award winner, two-time NBA All-Star, 50 Greatest EuroLeague Contributors, Olympic gold medalist in [2004](/wiki/Basketball_at_the_2004_Summer_Olympics_%E2%80%93_Men%27s_tournament "Basketball at the 2004 Summer Olympics – Men's tournament") with [Argentina](/wiki/Argentine_national_basketball_team "Argentine national basketball team").
* [Yao Ming](/wiki/Yao_Ming "Yao Ming"), China – 2016 inductee into the [Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame](/wiki/Naismith_Memorial_Basketball_Hall_of_Fame "Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame"), [first overall pick](/wiki/List_of_first_overall_NBA_draft_picks "List of first overall NBA draft picks") in the [2002 NBA draft](/wiki/2002_NBA_draft "2002 NBA draft") and eight-time NBA All-Star.
* [Leandro Barbosa](/wiki/Leandro_Barbosa "Leandro Barbosa"), Brazil – NBA champion with the Golden State Warriors (2015),[[210]](#cite_note-barbosachamp-211) 2007 Sixth Man Award winner.
* [Andrea Bargnani](/wiki/Andrea_Bargnani "Andrea Bargnani"), Italy – first overall pick in the [2006 NBA draft](/wiki/2006_NBA_draft "2006 NBA draft") by the [Toronto Raptors](/wiki/Toronto_Raptors "Toronto Raptors").
* [Giannis Antetokounmpo](/wiki/Giannis_Antetokounmpo "Giannis Antetokounmpo"), Greece – NBA champion with the [Milwaukee Bucks](/wiki/Milwaukee_Bucks "Milwaukee Bucks") (2021), [2021 NBA Finals](/wiki/2021_NBA_Finals "2021 NBA Finals") MVP, two-time NBA MVP, 2017 Most Improved Player, eight-time NBA All-Star.
* [Nikola Jokić](/wiki/Nikola_Joki%C4%87 "Nikola Jokić"), Serbia – NBA champion with the [Denver Nuggets](/wiki/Denver_Nuggets "Denver Nuggets") (2023), [2023 NBA Finals](/wiki/2023_NBA_Finals "2023 NBA Finals") MVP, three-time NBA MVP, six-time NBA All-Star, 2016 NBA All-Rookie First Team, Olympic silver medalist.
* [Luka Dončić](/wiki/Luka_Don%C4%8Di%C4%87 "Luka Dončić"), Slovenia – 2019 [NBA Rookie of the Year](/wiki/NBA_Rookie_of_the_Year_Award "NBA Rookie of the Year Award"), five-time [NBA All-Star](/wiki/NBA_All-Star "NBA All-Star"), European champion

On some occasions, young players, most but not all from the English-speaking world, have attended American colleges before playing in the NBA. Notable examples are:

* Nigerian [Hakeem Olajuwon](/wiki/Hakeem_Olajuwon "Hakeem Olajuwon") – first overall pick in the [1984 NBA draft](/wiki/1984_NBA_draft "1984 NBA draft"), two-time champion, 12-time NBA All-Star, 1994 NBA MVP, two-time NBA Finals MVP, two-time [NBA Defensive Player of the Year](/wiki/NBA_Defensive_Player_of_the_Year_Award "NBA Defensive Player of the Year Award") (only player to receive the MVP Award, Defensive Player of the Year Award, and Finals MVP award in the same season,) and Hall of Famer.

* Dutchman [Rik Smits](/wiki/Rik_Smits "Rik Smits") – second overall pick in the [1988 NBA draft](/wiki/1988_NBA_draft "1988 NBA draft"), 1998 NBA All-Star, played 12 years for the Indiana Pacers.
* German [Detlef Schrempf](/wiki/Detlef_Schrempf "Detlef Schrempf") – two-time NBA Sixth Man Award winner, three-time NBA All-Star.
* Canadians [Steve Nash](/wiki/Steve_Nash "Steve Nash") (two-time NBA MVP, eight-time NBA All-Star, Hall of Famer), [Andrew Wiggins](/wiki/Andrew_Wiggins "Andrew Wiggins") (first overall pick in the [2014 NBA draft](/wiki/2014_NBA_draft "2014 NBA draft"), 2015 [NBA Rookie of the Year](/wiki/NBA_Rookie_of_the_Year_Award "NBA Rookie of the Year Award")), and [Shai Gilgeous-Alexander](/wiki/Shai_Gilgeous-Alexander "Shai Gilgeous-Alexander") (2025 NBA scoring leader, NBA MVP, and [2025 NBA Finals](/wiki/2025_NBA_Finals "2025 NBA Finals") MVP)
* Australians [Luc Longley](/wiki/Luc_Longley "Luc Longley") (three-time champion with the Chicago Bulls), [Andrew Bogut](/wiki/Andrew_Bogut "Andrew Bogut") (first overall pick in the [2005 NBA draft](/wiki/2005_NBA_draft "2005 NBA draft"), 2015 NBA champion with Golden State Warriors) and [Ben Simmons](/wiki/Ben_Simmons "Ben Simmons") (first overall pick in the [2016 NBA draft](/wiki/2016_NBA_draft "2016 NBA draft"), 2018 [NBA Rookie of the Year](/wiki/NBA_Rookie_of_the_Year_Award "NBA Rookie of the Year Award"), three-time NBA All-Star).
* Sudanese-born Englishman [Luol Deng](/wiki/Luol_Deng "Luol Deng") – 2007 [NBA Sportsmanship Award](/wiki/NBA_Sportsmanship_Award "NBA Sportsmanship Award") winner, two-time NBA All-Star.
* Cameroonians [Joel Embiid](/wiki/Joel_Embiid "Joel Embiid") (2023 NBA MVP, four-time NBA All-Star, 2017 NBA All-Rookie First Team) and [Pascal Siakam](/wiki/Pascal_Siakam "Pascal Siakam") (2019 NBA champion with Toronto Raptors, 2019 Most Improved Player, two-time NBA All-Star)

#### Female coaches

See also: [List of female NBA coaches](/wiki/List_of_female_NBA_coaches "List of female NBA coaches")

[Becky Hammon](/wiki/Becky_Hammon "Becky Hammon") was hired by the [San Antonio Spurs](/wiki/San_Antonio_Spurs "San Antonio Spurs") on August 5, 2014, as an assistant coach, becoming the second female coach in NBA history, but the first full-time coach.[[211]](#cite_note-212)[[212]](#cite_note-borntocoach-213) She was also the first full-time female coach in any of the [four major professional sports leagues](/wiki/Major_professional_sports_leagues_in_the_United_States_and_Canada "Major professional sports leagues in the United States and Canada") in North America.[[212]](#cite_note-borntocoach-213)

"The NBA has conducted almost 4,200 tests for steroids and performance-enhancing drugs in the six seasons since these substances were banned by our drug program, and have had a total of 23 initial laboratory positives – approximately ½ of 1 percent. Of the 23 initial laboratory positives in the NBA's program, only three satisfied the additional criteria that must be met for a sample to be confirmed as "positive" under the NBA's drug program (i.e., a laboratory positive on the "B" sample conducted at a different laboratory, and review and confirmation by the Medical Review Officer). Several initial laboratory positives involved players that were terminated from employment prior to confirmation of their test results; others were found by the Medical Review Officer to be subject to a reasonable medical explanation. Each of the 3 players with a confirmed positive test result was immediately suspended. None of these players are currently playing in the NBA."
